TARGET AUDIENCE: female South Asian cardiology fellow

A close-up environmental portrait of a female South Asian cardiology fellow, writing clinical notes in a paper notebook at a sunlit home desk, stethoscope resting on the table beside her. This image will be used as a LinkedIn ad targeting female South Asian cardiology fellow — the subject must immediately read as a credible, senior female South Asian cardiology fellow professional in their authentic work environment.

COMPOSITION (frame the subject so there is clear background space for a text overlay):
- Place the subject in the LEFT or RIGHT half of the frame — NOT dead-centre. Leave the   opposite half mostly clear (background only, no subject pixels) so a 1-2 line headline   and a short subheadline can be composited next to the subject without overlap.
- The empty half should show natural background (bookshelves, plants, walls, soft   ambient light) with mid-tone to dark colour values so white text reads against it.
- CRITICAL HAIR/HEAD PLACEMENT: the TOP of the subject's head/hair (including flyaways)   must sit AT OR BELOW the 35% line from the top of the frame. The full upper 35% of   the canvas must be PURE BACKGROUND — zero hair, zero head, zero shoulders, zero   forehead. A headline overlay is composited into the top 8-30% region; if any subject   pixel intrudes there the ad is rejected. Frame the portrait LOWER than a typical   headshot — body/torso visible from waist up, head naturally sitting mid-low in the   frame. Do NOT place the head near the top edge.

BACKGROUND:
- Top 35% of the frame MUST be (a) pure background with NO subject pixels and (b)   mid-tone to dark (warm wood shelves, plant foliage, shadowed walls). No bright   white walls or blown-out windows in this zone — white headline text will overlay   it and needs contrast to read.
- Bottom third must also be mid-tone/darker for white subheadline text readability.
- Setting: lush home interior — bookshelves, potted plants, wall art, warm natural   window light. 85mm prime lens, shallow depth of field. thoughtful expression, looking slightly off to the side, three-quarter angle. Shot on film,   analog color grade, authentic lifestyle photo. NOT a stock photo. NOT a corporate headshot.
- COLOR MOOD: Warm pinkish-amber light in the top-left of the frame (as if a warm lamp   sits just outside the frame top-left). Cool teal/blue ambient light in the bottom-left   corner (evening window light or ambient fill). This natural lighting palette will be   enhanced by branded color overlays added in post-processing.
- LAPTOPS / SCREENS: prefer to keep them OUT of frame or out of focus. If a   laptop is part of the subject's activity, render it CLOSED, ANGLED AWAY from   the camera so the screen is not visible, or DEEPLY BLURRED in the bokeh —   NEVER showing software UI, document text, code, emails, video timelines, or   any rendered content. Preferred alternatives: paper notes, a notebook + pen,   the subject looking up away from the device, a cup of coffee on the desk.   (Updated 2026-05-20 — Vision QC's no-text-in-photo rule was failing every   attempt when laptops rendered with editing UI / document content.)

CRITICAL OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS:
- DO NOT render any text, words, letters, numbers, logos, or wordmarks in the image.
- DO NOT add any Outlier branding — logo, earnings strip, and gradient overlays are   composited separately in post-processing.
- OUTPUT ONLY THE CLEAN PHOTOGRAPH — no text overlays, no graphic elements, no   solid-color borders.

Apply now

- Place the subject in the LEFT or RIGHT half of the frame — NOT dead-centre. Leave the   opposite half mostly clear (background only, no subject pixels) so a 1-2 line headline   and a short subheadline can be composited next to the subject without overlap.
- The empty half should show natural background (bookshelves, plants, walls, soft   ambient light) with mid-tone to dark colour values so white text reads against it.
- CRITICAL HAIR/HEAD PLACEMENT: the TOP of the subject's head/hair (including flyaways)   must sit AT OR BELOW the 35% line from the top of the frame. The full upper 35% of   the canvas must be PURE BACKGROUND — zero hair, zero head, zero shoulders, zero   forehead. A headline overlay is composited into the top 8-30% region; if any subject   pixel intrudes there the ad is rejected. Frame the portrait LOWER than a typical   headshot — body/torso visible from waist up, head naturally sitting mid-low in the   frame. Do NOT place the head near the top edge.

BACKGROUND:
- Top 35% of the frame MUST be (a) pure background with NO subject pixels and (b)   mid-tone to dark (warm wood shelves, plant foliage, shadowed walls). No bright   white walls or blown-out windows in this zone — white headline text will overlay   it and needs contrast to read.
- Bottom third must also be mid-tone/darker for white subheadline text readability.
- Setting: lush home interior — bookshelves, potted plants, wall art, warm natural   window light. 85mm prime lens, shallow depth of field. genuine relaxed smile, looking directly at camera. Shot on film,   analog color grade, authentic lifestyle photo. NOT a stock photo. NOT a corporate headshot.
- COLOR MOOD: Warm pinkish-amber light in the top-left of the frame (as if a warm lamp   sits just outside the frame top-left). Cool teal/blue ambient light in the bottom-left   corner (evening window light or ambient fill). This natural lighting palette will be   enhanced by branded color overlays added in post-processing.
- LAPTOPS / SCREENS: prefer to keep them OUT of frame or out of focus. If a   laptop is part of the subject's activity, render it CLOSED, ANGLED AWAY from   the camera so the screen is not visible, or DEEPLY BLURRED in the bokeh —   NEVER showing software UI, document text, code, emails, video timelines, or   any rendered content. Preferred alternatives: paper notes, a notebook + pen,   the subject looking up away from the device, a cup of coffee on the desk.   (Updated 2026-05-20 — Vision QC's no-text-in-photo rule was failing every   attempt when laptops rendered with editing UI / document content.)

- DO NOT render any text, words, letters, numbers, logos, or wordmarks in the image.

- OUTPUT ONLY THE CLEAN PHOTOGRAPH — no text overlays, no graphic elements, no   solid-color borders.

gemini_prompt:
PHOTO GENERATION PROMPT:

TARGET AUDIENCE: female Vietnamese professional in her late 20s

A close-up environmental portrait of a female Vietnamese professional in her late 20s, sitting at a sunlit home desk reviewing printed notes with a pen, warm tropical light, relaxed home office. This image will be used as a LinkedIn ad targeting female Vietnamese professional in her late 20s — the subject must immediately read as a credible, senior female Vietnamese professional in her late 20s professional in their authentic work environment.

PROFESSION VISIBILITY (REQUIRED): the subject must be instantly recognizable as their stated profession (from the subject description above) through concrete visual cues — profession-specific attire (e.g. a physician in a white coat or scrubs with a stethoscope; an engineer in technical gear; a lawyer in formal business dress) AND/OR a signature tool or artifact of the trade visibly in frame (e.g. a clinician with an ECG printout, medical imaging, or a stethoscope). A generic person in plain casual clothes is NOT acceptable — a viewer must identify the profession at a glance. Keep these cues even in a relaxed/remote/home setting (e.g. a doctor at home still wears a white coat or has a stethoscope nearby).

COMPOSITION (frame the subject so there is clear background space for a text overlay):
- Place the subject in the LEFT or RIGHT half of the frame — NOT dead-centre. Leave the   opposite half mostly clear (background only, no subject pixels) so a 1-2 line headline   and a short subheadline can be composited next to the subject without overlap.
- The empty half should show natural background (bookshelves, plants, walls, soft   ambient light) with mid-tone to dark colour values so white text reads against it.
- CRITICAL HAIR/HEAD PLACEMENT: the TOP of the subject's head/hair (including flyaways)   must sit AT OR BELOW the 35% line from the top of the frame. The full upper 35% of   the canvas must be PURE BACKGROUND — zero hair, zero head, zero shoulders, zero   forehead. A headline overlay is composited into the top 8-30% region; if any subject   pixel intrudes there the ad is rejected. Frame the portrait LOWER than a typical   headshot — body/torso visible from waist up, head naturally sitting mid-low in the   frame. Do NOT place the head near the top edge.

BACKGROUND:
- Top 35% of the frame MUST be (a) pure background with NO subject pixels and (b)   mid-tone to dark (warm wood shelves, plant foliage, shadowed walls). No bright   white walls or blown-out windows in this zone — white headline text will overlay   it and needs contrast to read.
- Bottom third must also be mid-tone/darker for white subheadline text readability.
- Setting: lush home interior — bookshelves, potted plants, wall art, warm natural   window light. 85mm prime lens, shallow depth of field. thoughtful expression, looking slightly off to the side, three-quarter angle. Shot on film,   analog color grade, authentic lifestyle photo. NOT a stock photo. NOT a corporate headshot.
- COLOR MOOD: Warm pinkish-amber light in the top-left of the frame (as if a warm lamp   sits just outside the frame top-left). Cool teal/blue ambient light in the bottom-left   corner (evening window light or ambient fill). This natural lighting palette will be   enhanced by branded color overlays added in post-processing.
- LAPTOPS / SCREENS: prefer to keep them OUT of frame or out of focus. If a   laptop is part of the subject's activity, render it CLOSED, ANGLED AWAY from   the camera so the screen is not visible, or DEEPLY BLURRED in the bokeh —   NEVER showing software UI, document text, code, emails, video timelines, or   any rendered content. Preferred alternatives: paper notes, a notebook + pen,   the subject looking up away from the device, a cup of coffee on the desk.   (Updated 2026-05-20 — Vision QC's no-text-in-photo rule was failing every   attempt when laptops rendered with editing UI / document content.)

CRITICAL OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS:
- DO NOT render any text, words, letters, numbers, logos, or wordmarks in the image.
- DO NOT add any Outlier branding — logo, earnings strip, and gradient overlays are   composited separately in post-processing.
- OUTPUT ONLY THE CLEAN PHOTOGRAPH — no text overlays, no graphic elements, no   solid-color borders.
photo_subject: female Vietnamese professional in her late 20s, sitting at a sunlit home desk reviewing printed notes with a pen, warm tropical light, relaxed home office

inmail_body:
Thousands of German-speaking contributors are already applying their expertise to improve AI systems on Outlier, earning $29/hr for focused, substantive work.

On Outlier, you review and evaluate AI-generated text in German, rating accuracy, fluency, and reasoning quality. Your native command of the language and cultural context is precisely what AI developers cannot replicate with automation.

You set your own hours, work entirely remotely, and we send payment weekly. There are no fixed shifts and no long-term commitment strongly encouraged to get started.

If your background includes strong written German and subject-matter knowledge in any discipline, we match you to tasks that fit your expertise. Become familiar with project guidelines at your own pace and contribute on your schedule.

inmail_body:
Thousands of Bengali-speaking contributors are already earning USD on Outlier, reviewing and evaluating AI-generated content to help improve the models behind today's most-used AI tools.

Your language skills and domain knowledge are exactly what AI developers need. You review AI outputs, rate responses for accuracy and clarity, and generate content, all in Bengali and English, entirely remotely.

gemini_prompt:
PHOTO GENERATION PROMPT:

TARGET AUDIENCE: male white cardiology fellow

A close-up environmental portrait of a male white cardiology fellow, relaxing on a couch in home clothes, flipping through a printed medical journal, a closed laptop on the coffee table nearby. This image will be used as a LinkedIn ad targeting male white cardiology fellow — the subject must immediately read as a credible, senior male white cardiology fellow professional in their authentic work environment.

COMPOSITION (frame the subject so there is clear background space for a text overlay):
- Place the subject in the LEFT or RIGHT half of the frame — NOT dead-centre. Leave the   opposite half mostly clear (background only, no subject pixels) so a 1-2 line headline   and a short subheadline can be composited next to the subject without overlap.
- The empty half should show natural background (bookshelves, plants, walls, soft   ambient light) with mid-tone to dark colour values so white text reads against it.
- CRITICAL HAIR/HEAD PLACEMENT: the TOP of the subject's head/hair (including flyaways)   must sit AT OR BELOW the 35% line from the top of the frame. The full upper 35% of   the canvas must be PURE BACKGROUND — zero hair, zero head, zero shoulders, zero   forehead. A headline overlay is composited into the top 8-30% region; if any subject   pixel intrudes there the ad is rejected. Frame the portrait LOWER than a typical   headshot — body/torso visible from waist up, head naturally sitting mid-low in the   frame. Do NOT place the head near the top edge.

BACKGROUND:
- Top 35% of the frame MUST be (a) pure background with NO subject pixels and (b)   mid-tone to dark (warm wood shelves, plant foliage, shadowed walls). No bright   white walls or blown-out windows in this zone — white headline text will overlay   it and needs contrast to read.
- Bottom third must also be mid-tone/darker for white subheadline text readability.
- Setting: lush home interior — bookshelves, potted plants, wall art, warm natural   window light. 85mm prime lens, shallow depth of field. genuine relaxed smile, looking directly at camera. Shot on film,   analog color grade, authentic lifestyle photo. NOT a stock photo. NOT a corporate headshot.
- COLOR MOOD: Warm pinkish-amber light in the top-left of the frame (as if a warm lamp   sits just outside the frame top-left). Cool teal/blue ambient light in the bottom-left   corner (evening window light or ambient fill). This natural lighting palette will be   enhanced by branded color overlays added in post-processing.
- LAPTOPS / SCREENS: prefer to keep them OUT of frame or out of focus. If a   laptop is part of the subject's activity, render it CLOSED, ANGLED AWAY from   the camera so the screen is not visible, or DEEPLY BLURRED in the bokeh —   NEVER showing software UI, document text, code, emails, video timelines, or   any rendered content. Preferred alternatives: paper notes, a notebook + pen,   the subject looking up away from the device, a cup of coffee on the desk.   (Updated 2026-05-20 — Vision QC's no-text-in-photo rule was failing every   attempt when laptops rendered with editing UI / document content.)

CRITICAL OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS:
- DO NOT render any text, words, letters, numbers, logos, or wordmarks in the image.
- DO NOT add any Outlier branding — logo, earnings strip, and gradient overlays are   composited separately in post-processing.
- OUTPUT ONLY THE CLEAN PHOTOGRAPH — no text overlays, no graphic elements, no   solid-color borders.
photo_subject: male white cardiology fellow, relaxing on a couch in home clothes, flipping through a printed medical journal, a closed laptop on the coffee table nearby
